purpose: Seed the repeatable classic-arcade ingestion pipeline for a small Galaxian-style formation-entry and first-dive slice.

candidate_sources.title: Galaxian | 1979 | Arcade | Gameplay | HD 720p 60FPS
candidate_sources.license_or_usage_note: External gameplay video; use as review/reference only until download and reuse permissions are clarified.
candidate_sources.notes: Search result describes emulated arcade version captured at 60fps on default arcade difficulty. Good first candidate for formation-entry and first-dive timing if frame quality is stable.

candidate_sources.title: Arcade Archives Galaxian gameplay
candidate_sources.creator_or_archive: Nintendo Everything summary of Hamster Arcade Archiver stream
candidate_sources.duration: stream window starts around 3:30:41 per article
candidate_sources.license_or_usage_note: External article and embedded stream reference; use as citation and manual review source unless separate permission exists.
candidate_sources.notes: Useful because it points to a publisher-hosted Arcade Archives gameplay segment and summarizes core mechanics.

candidate_sources.title: Arcade Archives GALAXIAN
candidate_sources.license_or_usage_note: Use as citation for official release and high-level mechanic descriptions; not a media extraction source.
candidate_sources.notes: Official description names Galaxip, eerie-curving aliens, assault score doubling, Galboss, Red Alien escort order, and large formation destruction.

candidate_sources.title: Galaxian, Arcade Video game by NAMCO, Ltd. (1979)
candidate_sources.license_or_usage_note: Secondary reference; use for cross-checking, not as sole implementation authority.
candidate_sources.notes: Useful for enemy family naming, dive-bomb behavior, and groups of one or three attackers.

candidate_sources.title: Galaxian Videos for Arcade Games
candidate_sources.license_or_usage_note: Discovery index only; follow through to individual sources before analysis.
candidate_sources.notes: Useful for locating longer 60fps captures and longplays after the first seed window is ready.

selected_windows.why_it_matters: This window should capture the first playable formation entry, rack completion, first alien dive, player movement, and first shot cadence needed for the scout-wave slice.
analysis_questions: How long does the formation take to settle?
When does the first dive begin relative to formation completion?
Does the first dive involve one alien or an escort group?
How strict does the single-shot cadence feel in the first exchange?
How much lateral correction does the player use before the first shot?
selected_windows.notes: Exact timestamps are intentionally pending until the source is locally reviewed or replaced by a reproducible emulator capture.

next_steps: Confirm whether the first candidate video can be locally clipped for analysis.
If not, create a local emulator capture with stable frame rate and documented settings.
Generate contact sheet and first-pass event log.
Promote high-confidence observations into the semantic scout-wave profile.
